title: Barristers and judges
description: Represent clients in criminal and civil litigation and other legal proceedings, draw up legal documents, or manage or advise clients on legal transactions. May specialize in a single area or may practice broadly in many areas of law. Assist judges in court or by conducting research or preparing legal documents. Conduct hearings to recommend or make decisions on claims concerning government programs or other government-related matters. Determine liability, sanctions, or penalties, or recommend the acceptance or rejection of claims or settlements. (Based on 11 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Prepare written opinions and decisions on cases, including rules on admissibility of evidence and methods of conducting testimony, and analyze facts and issues from documents such as pleadings and motions.

Conduct hearings to preside over cases, direct juries to deduce facts from evidence presented, listen to allegations made by plaintiffs, and monitor proceedings to ensure rules and procedures are followed.
Research and analyze laws, regulations, policies, and precedent decisions to prepare for hearings, advise attorneys, and write opinions on legal issues concerning cases.

Represent clients in court or before government agencies, advising them on their legal rights and obligations while negotiating settlements of civil disputes and settling disputes between opposing attorneys.
Evaluate evidence and claims, determining liability and recommending the acceptance or rejection of claims, settlements, or compromise offers according to laws and regulations.
Supervise court staff, maintain order in the courtroom, and enforce courtroom rules of behavior while escorting judges and prisoners during proceedings.
Conduct preliminary hearings to decide on issues such as custody, issue subpoenas, and administer oaths in preparation for formal hearings or to testify at court trials.
Conduct investigations, gathering and analyzing information related to cases, or interviewing claimants and witnesses to obtain evidence for legal proceedings.
Draft, prepare, and review legal documents, including wills, contracts, and legal briefs, while keeping abreast of changes in the law to inform judges and aid in case management.
Confer with disputants and attorneys to clarify issues and develop procedural strategies, while participating in discussions between trial attorneys and judges throughout the case process.
Read court documents and legal data, such as motions and pleadings, to ascertain facts, assess issues involved, and conduct studies for compliance with legal requirements.
Administer judicial tasks such as preparing reports, maintaining court records, reviewing dockets, and providing assistance to the public regarding legal processes and proceedings.
Impose restrictions on parties in civil cases until trials can be held and ensure that all applicable rules and procedures are adhered to throughout the proceedings.
Issue arrest warrants, review documents for legal accuracy, and verify land-related documents for their compliance with legal standards.
Grant divorces, rule on custody disputes, and enforce court orders regarding legal rights, obligations, and support of children in cases before the court as required.
Conduct personal background investigations or assess claims related to issues such as character, financial status, or personal history as part of investigative duties in legal cases.
Provide information to the public on legal issues and judicial systems via public speeches, media, and conducting studies related to legal procedures.
Facilitate settlement discussions through mediation techniques and organize meetings to negotiate civil disputes between parties involved in cases.
Conduct studies of appeals procedures and assess compliance with legal requirements to ensure that processes are appropriately followed and documented.
Perform wedding ceremonies and participate in judicial tribunals to resolve various disputes and uphold legal decisions as needed.
Prepare, edit, or review legal documents, including legislation, briefs, pleadings, appeals, wills, contracts, and real estate closing statements.

tools_used.name: Universal serial bus USB flash drives
